goal: Define the supported local-agent installation and verification contract.
description: Scope: docs/plan/installer-adaptation/Requirements.md; parent is the user's request and children are evidence, validation, and implementation work. Context: the local audit found Windows-only installation, configuration-only checks, and a Claude warning. Target: record REQ-001 through REQ-005, target users, scope, non-goals, and a final acceptance target. Design Considerations: distinguish supported clients from arbitrary third-party tools and keep one current skill tree per resolved runtime. Design Value: a stable contract prevents a platform-specific patch from silently weakening existing update behavior. Constraints & Risks: Docker is diagnostic-only and Windows/WSL must remain separate runtime targets.

acceptance_criteria.text: Requirements.md defines REQ-001 through REQ-005, users, scope, non-goals, and acceptance target.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Requirements.md defines the five requirements, target users, scope, non-goals, and acceptance target from the local installer audit.

goal: Ground installer changes in the observed runtime and code-path gaps.
description: Scope: docs/plan/installer-adaptation/Evidence.md, scripts/install.py, and tests/test_install_tool.py. Context: requirements define the desired final state. Target: cite the current Path.home routing, WSL probe-only behavior, structural-only checks, and Claude manifest warning. Design Considerations: select a reusable WSL runtime value object and table-driven native validator only where they reduce repeated command policy. Design Value: evidence keeps deployment and verification changes tied to observed behavior. Constraints & Risks: do not expose user-specific runtime data in repository artifacts.

acceptance_criteria.text: Evidence.md maps each current gap to source behavior and records bounded design decisions.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Evidence.md maps current source behavior to the WSL deployment, runtime validation, and Claude manifest decisions.

goal: Map every installer requirement to reproducible automated and local checks.
description: Scope: docs/plan/installer-adaptation/Validation.md. Context: requirements and evidence establish the target and current gaps. Target: map each REQ label to unit tests and available local client checks before code changes. Design Considerations: test WSL behavior through mocked command boundaries plus real local smoke checks when present. Design Value: the matrix distinguishes installation evidence from runtime-load evidence. Constraints & Risks: optional CLIs must not make a structurally valid install fail merely because their executable is absent.

acceptance_criteria.text: Validation.md maps every REQ label to concrete tests or runtime checks.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Validation.md maps REQ-001 through REQ-005 to unit and local runtime verification.

goal: Fix the installer module boundaries and contracts before adapting runtimes.
description: Scope: docs/plan/installer-adaptation/Architecture.md and the inspected installer interfaces. Context: source evidence requires WSL deployment and optional runtime checks without duplicated logic. Target: define the WSL runtime contract, validation result policy, module ownership, and dependency direction. Design Considerations: reuse existing copy and adapter primitives; isolate subprocess execution behind current command helpers. Design Value: explicit contracts keep Windows, WSL, and optional-client paths testable. Constraints & Risks: preserve CLI argument compatibility and one-current-implementation removal behavior.

acceptance_criteria.text: Architecture.md records module map, contracts, dependency direction, and chosen patterns.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Architecture.md defines the WSL runtime contract, runtime validation policy, module map, and dependency direction.

goal: Deliver runtime-aware WSL deployment, native validation, and a complete Claude manifest.
description: Scope: scripts/install.py, tests/test_install_tool.py, and README.md; owns installer runtime discovery/deployment/doctor code and tests, consuming the contracts in Architecture.md. Context: Windows cannot install to detected WSL OpenCode, optional clients have structural-only checks, and Claude warns about a missing version. Target: satisfy REQ-001 through REQ-005 in the single installer implementation. Design Considerations: use structured runtime data, argument-list subprocess calls, safely quoted WSL scripts, atomic skill replacement, and table-driven optional validators. Design Value: the changes make discovery actionable and diagnostics meaningful without duplicate adapter flows. Constraints & Risks: do not retain compatibility shims, mutate unrelated user configuration, or turn missing optional CLIs into failures.

acceptance_criteria.text: WSL OpenCode deployment and verification, optional client validation, Claude manifest version, documentation, and unit coverage are implemented.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Implemented WSL OpenCode deployment and agent-list checks, optional CLI checks, Gemini extension validation/listing, Claude manifest version, documentation, and unit coverage.

acceptance_criteria.text: Existing shared/native resolution and duplicate-removal behavior remains covered by tests.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Existing resolution, duplicate-removal, uninstall, managed-config, and CLI override tests pass in the full suite.

commit.message: feat: complete local agent installer adaptation

goal: Prove the installer meets every runtime-adaptation requirement end to end.
description: Scope: full test suite, installer doctor, available native client validators, WSL OpenCode agent listing, and generated skill digests. Context: implementation delivers the planned installer behavior. Target: prove REQ-001 through REQ-005 using the Validation.md mapping. Design Considerations: distinguish unavailable optional CLIs from failing configured runtimes. Design Value: end-to-end evidence prevents an apparently valid file layout from masking an unloaded adapter. Constraints & Risks: report environmental limitations explicitly rather than claiming unsupported clients were exercised.

acceptance_criteria.text: All mapped automated tests and available local runtime checks pass.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: 48 automated tests passed; real Windows update deployed WSL Debian; doctor reports WSL OpenCode OK; Claude plugin validation passed; Cursor CLI validation passed; Copilot and Gemini CLIs are unavailable and therefore reported as structural-install warnings.

acceptance_criteria.text: Manifest workspace validates and reports a completed plan state.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: manifest_tool validate, sync-plan, and status complete successfully for docs/plan.
